EV = $711M x 17.0x = $12,087M. 17x comp: premium subscription-data peer set — TransUnion ~14x, Verisk ~25x, parent SPGI ~20x EV/EBITDA; CARFAX's near-monopoly VIN-history data + 81% recurring rev supports a mid-to-high-teens multiple. Net debt: $2,000M new senior notes ($650M@5.05% '29 + $650M@5.45% '31 + $700M@6.05% '36) less ~$200M retained cash = $1,800M (Form 10, pro-forma Mar-31-2026 cap table; $1,900M net cash dividend up to parent). Shares 295.077M (1:1 distribution on 295,077,160 SPGI shares as of May-15-2026). FV=(12,087-1,800-0)/295.1=$34.86. reference_price $30.00 = ESTIMATED when-issued first-trade (MBGL not yet trading; record Jun-15, WI ~Jun-26, distribution/NYSE Jul-1-2026); modest discount to fair for a newly-levered sub-scale orphan. reference_rr 0.68 at that estimate. Floor = de-rated no-deal standalone at 12x (levered sub-scale info orphan, forced-seller index pressure): (711x12-1800)/295.1=$22.81.

valuation.valuation_basis: DEMOTED. The Delivery-Hero/forced-seller framing was the wrong read: the DHER-overhang-clearing catalyst re-rates the UNDERLYING (DHER), NOT Prosus the compelled seller, so there is no event-driven re-rate that accrues to PRX from selling DHER (it sold 4.5% to Uber for ~EUR270m Apr'26 - a rounding error vs the holdco). PRX is therefore valued as a Tencent-proxy holdco on NAV-discount mechanics, not a special-situation. Live EUR39.52 sits at a ~35% discount to NAV; NAV/sh ~EUR60.8 (Tencent = 76% of GAV, ~22.8% of Tencent / 2,066m shrs; equities123/Prosus IR 2026). The only real 'catalyst' is the self-funded buyback narrowing the discount (54% Tencent-funded since Jan'26). Not a clean special-situation comp -> floor WIDENED to the 52wk low EUR37.37 (Tencent-beta downside; PRX NAV moves ~$1.35B per 1% Tencent). fair_value EUR46.21 assumes discount narrows 35%->24% on continued buyback, consistent with Morgan Stanley OW ('29% upside, discount peaks'). R:R 3.1:1 (EUR6.69 up vs EUR2.16 down) but this is a Tencent/China-beta carry trade, NOT the event-driven asymmetry the book hunts; correctly demoted out of the catalyst sleeve.
valuation.instrument_ref: PRX.AS ordinary shares (Tencent-proxy holdco NAV)

valuation.valuation_basis: AdX-divestiture beneficiary, treated as a regulatory BINARY (overriding 'forced_seller' lane: MGNI is a beneficiary of a structural-share gift, not a compelled seller). Google ~60% SSP share, Magnite ~6%, PubMatic ~4% (ppc.land/StockStory 2026). Brinkema found liability Apr'25; remedies closing args Nov 21 2025; NO ruling yet as of Jun 8 2026 (Digiday/AdExchanger), judge has signaled skepticism on structural relief. WIN = AdX ordered divested + barred 10yrs: independent SSPs capture the freed 60% share at ~85-90% incremental margin (Magnite guided 90% incr; Crossroads Capital) -> EBITDA roughly doubles off ~$620M contribution-ex-TAC, re-rate to ~$27 (prior 52wk high $26.65 set on remedy optimism). LOSE = behavioral-only / multi-year appeal: stays a 13x-EBITDA SSP, de-rates to ~$11 (near 52wk low $10.82). FY25 rev $713.95M, Adj EBITDA ~$156M (EV/EBITDA 13.1x), net CASH ~$275M (ND/EBITDA -1.76); 142.6M sh; ref $14.61. Barbell EV at $14.61 (p=0.30) = +8.1%: 0.30*(+84.8%) + 0.70*(-24.7%); reward:risk 3.4:1. Net-cash + 13x standalone caps downside vs option-like upside; better than PUBM (still below 200d $16.0, 6% vs 4% share to compound onto).
valuation.prob_basis: ~30% structural-divestiture-that-survives-appeal probability: Brinkema repeatedly questioned structural remedies and timing ('window dressing' hearings, The Current/AdExchanger Sep-Nov'25), leaning behavioral; even a divest order faces years of appeals before share actually flows. 30% = calibrated joint prob of (order is structural) x (survives/implements) within horizon.

Adjustments (SIGNED) = +$1,125M trademark-license liability ('Honeywell' name, 60 x $18.75M monthly, <5yr) MINUS $2,727M net defined-benefit PENSION ASSET contributed to SpinCo = -$1,602M net (a net positive to equity). Shares 316.827M (1 HONA per 2 HON shares; 316,826,559 per Form 10 pro-forma). FV=(66,750-15,000-(-1,602))/316.8=$168.41. CORRECTION confirmed: funded debt is $16.0B (not the garbled $24B). reference_price $150.00 = ESTIMATED when-issued first-trade (HONA not yet trading; record Jun-15, distribution Jun-29-2026, NASDAQ); large clean A&D spin draws index/passive buying so a smaller discount to fair than MBGL. reference_rr 0.49. Floor = de-rated standalone at 11x (cyclical-OE roll, aero-cycle fear): (4450x11-15000+(1125-2727))/316.8=$112.22.

valuation.valuation_basis: ACTIVE LME (UK Restructuring Plan + Ch15; RSA signed 17-Mar-2026, 95-97%+ of ~$5.8B corp debt; terms in DEFR14A). Sequence: vote deadline 9-Jun -> annual meeting/vote 17-Jun -> plan meetings 15-Jun -> targeted close Q3-2026. Structure: NFE splits into creditor-owned private BrazilCo + publicly-traded 'New NFE' (LNG-to-power). New NFE corp debt cut ~$5.7B -> ~$527.5M new term loans; creditors also get up to $2.5B convertible preferred (3yr, PIK 3%/5%/7%) + 65% of New NFE common; EXISTING common falls to 35% at close. THE KILLER OVERHANG: the $2.5B pref MANDATORILY CONVERTS after 3yrs into 87% of fully-diluted equity -> on look-through, legacy common = 35% x 13% residual = ~4.55% of FD. Value: New NFE 2027E adj EBITDA ~$415M+ x ~8x = ~$3.32B EV - $527.5M term loans = ~$2.79B common pool; existing holders' 4.55% = ~$127M / 285.63M sh = ~$0.44/sh look-through. valuation.valuation_basis: Merger-arb COLLAR. IonQ buying SkyWater (US defense foundry). Each SKYT -> $15.00 cash + $20.00 IonQ stock; collar 0.3326 sh (if IonQ 20d-VWAP > $60.13) to 0.5265 sh (if < $37.99). IonQ live $63.58 is ABOVE the upper collar -> exchange ratio FIXED at 0.3326 -> stock leg now floats = 0.3326*63.58 = $21.15 -> current face (deal_price) = $15.00+$21.15 = $36.15. SKYT live $36.82 trades THROUGH face (-1.8% to face), so there is NEGATIVE arb carry — the book is long for (a) the floored downside: $15 cash locked + stock leg can only fall back to $20 unless IonQ < $37.99 (i.e. >=$35 face protected across the whole collar band), and (b) above-collar IonQ-beta optionality. FTC 2nd Request issued Apr 24 2026 on the vertical/national-security-adjacent combo (the open gate); target Q2/Q3 2026. break_prob 25% (2nd-request adds real timeline/condition risk). floor=undisturbed $25.36 (38% premium to SKYT 30-day VWAP as of 1/23/2026 implies ~$25.4 standalone). R:R to face = (36.15-36.82)/(36.82-25.36) = -0.06 — captured value is the collar floor + IonQ upside, NOT spread. Source: IonQ/SkyWater 8-Ks + IonQ press release (Jan 2026) + FTC 2nd-request 8-K (Apr 2026), live FMP 2026-06-08.

Because only ~25-30% of float is repurchased, the realized blend to a holder ~ 0.30*$1.20 (prorated tender) + 0.70*stub; residual_value (post-buyback stub level) ~$0.99 (reverts toward 50-day avg once the bid lifts, modestly accretion-helped by the ~25-30% share-count cut). fair_value_target SET to the tender ceiling $1.20 (NOT $1.20+residual, to avoid partial-tender double-count). downside_floor = ex-return $0.98 (the auction MINIMUM). reference_rr = (1.20-1.175)/(1.175-0.98)=0.13 — a near-dated, low-risk small return (the lone survivor): upside capped at $1.20, floored at $0.98, T-2 days. Source: Expensify 8-K + SC TO-I + IR/BusinessWire release (May 13 2026), live FMP 2026-06-08.

valuation.valuation_basis: OVERRIDE of merger_arb lane: this is an RMT spin, not a takeout of MOD. Per the Jan-29-2026 agreement (8-K) Modine carves its Performance Technologies segment into Gentherm (THRM); MOD holders KEEP RemainCo Modine (Climate Solutions) and ALSO receive ~21M THRM shares, and Modine receives a $210M cash distribution. So MOD post-deal value = RemainCo + THRM stake, NOT a single per-share consideration. RemainCo = Climate Solutions (FY2026 adj EBITDA $377.1M, sales $2,062M; Data Centers +60-80%/HVAC +5-10% into FY2027) less ~$50M retained corporate -> ~$420M FY2027E EBITDA at 22.0x (rich data-center thermal, between nVent ~17x and Vertiv ~28x) = $9,240M. THRM stake = 21M sh x $36.77 live = $772M. Net debt $362.8M (mgmt) less $210M cash in = $153M; +$120M retained cap leases. 53M sh (52.82M out, 10-K). Build=(10,012-153-120)/53=$183.75. HONEST READ: MOD at $275 (52wk low $86 -> +220%) already prices RemainCo at ~33x fwd EBITDA; even a generous SoP sits well below spot -> NO upside / catalyst over-discounted. PT contributed at ~6.8x $147M post-synergy EBITDA = $1.0B EV / $790M equity (deal terms).

valuation.valuation_basis: Dated forward binary on the mid-2026 Phase 1/2 NGN-401 interim readout (>=12-mo follow-up on all 10 pediatric/adolescent participants); Embolden registrational dosing completed 2026-06-07 with 25 dosed (vs 24 ITT target), Embolden topline not until 2H-2027, BLA after. WIN ($42): a clean, durable 12-mo efficacy+safety readout re-rates above the 52-wk high ($37.27) as the Rett gene-therapy opportunity (no approved disease-modifying Rx) de-risks into registration; BTD already granted on the Oct-30-2025 cut (88% CGI-I improving, 35 milestones, durable multidomain gains). LOSS ($12): durability fade / SAE / endpoint ambiguity collapses toward the ~$7.28/sh net-cash floor (cash+ST inv $243.2M, net cash ~$115M / 15.80M sh) plus residual fully-dosed-registrational + platform optionality. BARBELL not a ratio. Sources: Neurogene Q1-2026 release (SEC, 2026-05-12), Embolden dosing-complete PR (StockTitan, 2026-06-07), BTD announcement (BioSpace/IR).
valuation.prob_basis: Calibrated ~0.60 for a clearly stock-re-rating positive interim: high base rate that the mid-2026 update CONFIRMS, since FDA Breakthrough Therapy was already granted on this exact program's earlier interim cut and the construct uses miRNA-regulated EXACT tech to avoid the MECP2-overexpression toxicity that sank prior Rett gene-therapy attempts; haircut from PDUFA-grade certainty because this is an open-label, single-arm, n=10 12-mo durability readout (real fade/safety/endpoint-interpretation variance), not an approval. Sources: Neurogene IR BTD release; Q1-2026 8-K.

The one correction to flag: the triage's "CCM" symbol is wrong for trading — CrossCountry is private, so the actual instrument is TWO.

valuation.valuation_basis: Contested merger-arb. Board-backed deal = CrossCountry $12.00/sh ALL-CASH + a small stub dividend (amended merger agreement; no financing contingency; 41 of 53 regulatory approvals secured); special meeting reconvenes 2026-06-11. Competing UWMC $12.50 (cash-or-2.3328 UWMC shares) was UNANIMOUSLY REJECTED by the TWO board as illusory because a stockholder failing to elect cash defaults to UWMC stock worth ~$7.58. Live price $12.345 already trades ABOVE the $12.00 committed cash, i.e. the market is pricing a topping/bump beyond the board deal. deal_price set at $12.25 (CrossCountry $12.00 + ~$0.25 stub, probability-weighted toward a modest topping vs UWMC's $12.50), undisturbed $9.92 (2026-12-16, day before the original UWMC bid). At $12.345 the arb to the committed deal is slightly NEGATIVE - you are paying up for the bidding-war outcome. NAV cushion: book value ~$14.5/sh (price/book ~0.85), so a break likely re-rates toward ~0.7x BV (~$10-11), not a crash.

STOCK DEAL => deal_price floats with acquirer: 0.6539 x EUR56.50 (AKZA.AS live) x 1.15406 EUR/USD = $42.64/sh. (At announce it implied $47.08 / +67% off $28.18; AKZA has since fallen ~16% from its EUR67.6 high, compressing the headline value.) undisturbed_price=$28.18 (pre-Nov-18-2025). valuation.valuation_basis: BBP-418 (LGMD2I/R9) PDUFA Nov 27 2026 under Priority Review (FDA acceptance PR May 27 2026); positive Ph3 FORTIFY interim = statistically significant 2.6-pt NSAD benefit vs placebo at 12mo. CRITICAL re-frame: BBIO is NOT a cash-barbell biotech — net debt ~$2.5B (royalty/term debt funding launch), negative book equity per FMP 3/31/26 BS (totalDebt $3.385B, cash+inv ~$1.00B). The DOWNSIDE FLOOR is therefore the standalone value of the ALREADY-APPROVED, launched acoramidis/Attruby ATTR-CM franchise (FY25 net rev $362.4M, Q1-26 rev $194.5M annualizing ~$700M+, bull peak >$3B) plus infigratinib (achondroplasia NDA Q3-26) — not cash/sh. valuation.valuation_basis: Single FORWARD binary: opakalim/BHV-7000 (selective Kv7.2/7.3 activator) topline from the FIRST of TWO pivotal Ph2/3 refractory-focal-epilepsy studies, 2H-2026. Supportive prior data: 54% achieved >=50% seizure reduction over 6mo, triple time-to-seizure, low CNS-AE / differentiated tolerability (>1000 subjects). Textbook single-readout-without-clear-mispricing: won't register on one study, and epilepsy adjunctive trials carry placebo-response variance. Cash $351.8M (3/31/26) / 105.865M sh = $3.32/sh — but BHVN retains multiple shots (Graves', IgAN, SMA-adjacent) so a miss does NOT cut to cash. win_prob 0.55 = Kv7 mechanism clinically validated (ezogabine precedent) + strong Ph2 effect, discounted for single-study + placebo risk + need to confirm in study 2. target_on_win $18 ~ FMP consensus median $18 (cons $17.6); downside_on_loss $6 (re-rates down hard but residual pipeline+cash floor >$3.32 cash). EV barbell: 0.55*(18-11.18)+0.45*(6-11.18)= +$1.42/sh (~+13% EV, but high variance). reference_rr (EV%)=(18-11.18)/(11.18-6)=1.32.
valuation.prob_basis: Kv7.2/7.3 mechanism de-risked by ezogabine/retigabine precedent + opakalim Ph2 efficacy (54% >=50% responders, well tolerated). Capped at 0.55 because this is FIRST of two registration studies (no filing on a single readout) and adjunctive focal-epilepsy placebo response is a known confounder.

catalyst_1.evidence: FMC Q1 2026 earnings call (early May 2026, via Investing.com/GuruFocus recaps): "by the end of Q2 [we] expect to have more clarity on a review of strategic options" â€” and FMC press release "FMC sets 2026 priorities and announces exploration of strategic options including but not limited to the sale of the company," Feb-4-2026 (PRNewswire/investors.fmc.com); spot $11.64 via FMP quote (2026-06-05).

catalyst_2.title: Milestone

catalyst_density.rating: M â€” real, dated, near-term binary (Q2 update ~6-8 wks out) with a large valuation gap and stock at 52-wk lows (optionality faded, not priced), but no named counterparty and a genuine Rynaxypyr post-patent / weak-interest-coverage overhang cap conviction.
catalyst_density.analysis: The Feb-4-2026 strategic review is genuine, specific and dated, and remains forward â€” not fired (no transaction) and the reverse of re-rated: FMC trades $11.64 at its 52-week low, below the Feb-announcement $13.67 and the post-Q1 $16.40 spike and far under its 50d/200d ($15.08/$19.73), so the market has faded the sale optionality. The hard catalyst is management's commitment to update the strategic-options review by end of Q2 2026 (Q2 earnings call), creating a defined binary with ~55-63% upside to consensus/fair-value if a credible bidder or deal emerges. It stays WATCH not ACTIVE because the process is explicitly preliminary with "no assurance," there is no named acquirer, and the Rynaxypyr post-patent revenue erosion (FY guide -5%, mid-single-digit price decline) plus weak interest coverage are a real fundamental offset and the reason for the de-rating.

valuation.valuation_basis: Single-segment (pure-play crop protection) take-out SoP. Feb-4-2026 board authorization to explore strategic options incl. sale of the company (FMC IR/8-K); CEO Brondeau in talks w/ 5-10 buyers (corporate + PE), per Motley Fool Apr-8-2026. 2026 guidance Adj EBITDA $670-730M (mid $700M, down 17% on price); revenue $3.60-3.80B (PRNewswire Q1 Apr-29-2026). Take-out: $700M EBITDA x 9.5x (slight premium to BASF 8.6x EV/EBITDA & below ag median 10.2x, control + new-molecule pipeline buyers want) = EV $6,650M - net debt $4,265.7M - minority $27.8M = $2,356.5M / 125.3M = $18.81. Floor = de-rated no-deal levered standalone on trough EBITDA $700M @ 7.0x (below peer median) = $4,900M - 4,265.7M - 27.8M = $606.5M / 125.3M = $4.84; the thin levered equity sliver means $11.085 already embeds heavy deal hope.

sop_components.basis: 2026 guide Adj EBITDA mid $700M; 9.5x take-out vs BASF 8.6x EV/EBITDA + ag median 10.2x (control premium + pipeline); buyers 5-10 per CEO Apr-2026

candidates.edge: M â€” real dated FDA binary ~7 months out with genuine label-expansion optionality, but the acceptance event already fired May 19 (stock now below its 50-day MA at $6.92, faded the pop), the QUILT-3.032 data is public/known, and there is a securities class-action overhang (Jan 19-Mar 24 2026 class period) that muddies the name.

candidates.analysis_summary: [SWEEP 9 (GVA-IMTX)] The sBLA acceptance is verified and confirms a hard, idiosyncratic, non-macro binary: FDA must rule by Jan 6, 2027 on extrapolating the ANKTIVA+BCG indication to BCG-unresponsive papillary NMIBC, a genuine unfired forward decision with first-in-class label-expansion upside. However, the acceptance announcement itself (May 19, 2026) is the fired leg, the supporting efficacy data is already public, and the stock has since faded below its 50-day average, so the unpriced edge is moderate rather than high. The clean dated forward binary justifies ACTIVE at score 6; the main risks are FDA's stated focus on whether papillary/CIS overlap adequately justifies extrapolation (CRL risk) and an unrelated litigation overhang.

catalyst_3.title: Verify
catalyst_3.evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE CONFIRMS: BusinessWire/PRNewswire/ImmunityBio.com press release dated 2026-05-19 â€” FDA accepted the sBLA for ANKTIVA+BCG in BCG-unresponsive NMIBC with papillary disease (without CIS), PDUFA action date Jan 6, 2027. This is a genuine UNFIRED FORWARD binary ~7 months out (not yet a decision), correctly distinct from the existing BCG-unresponsive CIS approval, expanding to ~85% of 64,000 annual NMIBC patients. FDA explicitly stated the review focus is whether CIS/papillary overlap adequately justifies extrapolation â€” i.e., the CRL risk the line names is real and accurate. Caveat (not disqualifying): this is a RESUBMISSION (resubmitted Mar 9, 2026 after FDA requested more efficacy/long-term data, no new trials) which modestly elevates CRL risk. Stock fade verified: $6.92, below 50-day avg $7.59 (-5.1% today), though still well above 200-day $4.87. Idiosyncratic, non-macro, dated, tradeable (13.5M vol, $7.2B cap, optionable); Jan/Feb 2027 LEAPS correctly straddle the binary. Self-assessment honestly flags fired-leg/public-data â†’ moderate (not high) edge. Score 6 / ACTIVE well-calibrated.

NOT a survival binary: ANKTIVA is ALREADY FDA-approved/commercial in CIS NMIBC -> drug, manufacturing & safety established; this is a regulatory extrapolation decision (CIS->papillary, QUILT-3.032 Cohort B + literature bridge). win_prob 0.55 = label-expansion approval rates ~70-80% for already-approved drugs, DISCOUNTED because efficacy rests on single-arm extrapolation not a randomized confirmatory trial (contested), but FDA's acceptance signals the bridge is reviewable. target_on_win $10.00 (papillary ~triples NMIBC TAM vs CIS-only; re-rate toward prior optimism, 52wk-high $12.43; ~1,047M sh). downside_on_loss $5.00 (CRL keeps CIS franchise + $380.9M cash/STI and a confirmatory-trial path; net debt $919M Oberland royalty). EV = .55*10 + .45*5 = $7.75 -> +8% vs ref $7.175 (mkt already prices most of it).
valuation.prob_basis: Label-expansion of an already-approved drug (base rate ~70-80%) discounted to 0.55 for extrapolation-based (non-randomized) efficacy bridge from CIS to papillary; FDA sBLA acceptance is a partial de-risk.

Twice-rejected: CRL Jul-21-2025 (FDA: single-arm IGNYTE 'not adequate & well-controlled,' heterogeneous population, IGNYTE-3 confirmatory design issues — a substantial-evidence problem, source: Dermatology Times / Scientific American Jul-2025) and a second setback Apr-2026. May-29-2026 (GlobeNewswire/8-K) FDA ALIGNED on resubmission of SAME dataset with prioritized/urgent review — driven by FDA-leadership/political will (Makary/Prasad unmet-need push), NOT new efficacy data. win_prob=0.50 CALIBRATED: pure-merits coin-flip-or-worse (same-data substantial-evidence risk unresolved) lifted by FDA's stated intent to prioritize; cross-checks to market-implied (at $8.905 with floor $2.25/target $16, implied p=(8.905-2.25)/(16-2.25)=0.48). target_on_win=$16 (commercial melanoma franchise, peak US sales ~$0.5-0.75B at ~2-3x peak on de-risked launch + pipeline; pre-CRL traded $10-15+). downside_on_loss=$2.25 = net cash/sh (cash+ST inv $269M, debt $76M, net cash ~$193M /82.6M sh ≈ $2.34 less ~6mo burn; note panic floor undershot to $1.50 52-wk low post-CRL).

catalyst_3.evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE CONFIRMS THE BINARY, CORRECTS THE TIMELINE. SELLAS' Q1-2026 release (GlobeNewsWire, 2026-05-12; IR-mirrored) states verbatim: "78 Events have Occurred in the Trial as of May 11, 2026; SELLAS Remains Blinded to Trial Outcomes" and "Final Analysis of REGAL Trial of GPS in AML to be Conducted Following the 80th Event." So the REGAL CR2-AML OS binary is real, dated, single-name (not macro), and as of early June 2026 still FORWARD/UNFIRED (80th event not yet reached; efficacy blinded) -> the FIRED-not-forward attack fails, holds ACTIVE. Live quote confirms triage figures: $8.23 (today -8.96%), yearLow $1.39, priceAvg200 $3.57, mktcap ~$1.17B. CORRECTION: the line's claim that "management says topline arrives within weeks of that trigger" is NOT supported by the primary source -- the 05-12 release gives no weeks-level timeline, only the customary "database lock... unblinding... disclosure of topline results" sequence, and the prior 12-29-2025 release explicitly cautions "the timing of studies with overall survival as an endpoint can vary." So the timing is event-driven and indeterminate, not "weeks." HIDDEN OVERHANG: a separate pending arbitration ruling on a GPS China-payment dispute is an undisclosed binary risk not noted in the line. Edge is genuinely Medium not High: stock has already run ~+50%+ off lows (priced optimism), and a ~12.6 vs 8.0-month OS bar makes the win/fail genuinely uncertain. Tradeable as a defined-risk long-call/long-straddle into elevated IV. Net: core binary CONFIRMED; "within weeks" timeline magnitude REFUTED -> CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, score 6.

valuation.valuation_basis: Binary on FDA feedback (expected mid-2026, guided in Apr 28, 2026 Q1 update) on a potential accelerated-approval pathway for WVE-006 in alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency (AATD), after regaining full rights from GSK (Feb 2, 2026). This is a SOFT binary (pathway feedback, not an approval decision). Positive RestorAATion-2: wild-type M-AAT = 64% of total AAT, Z-AAT reduced 71%, 11.9-13.6 uM total AAT. win_prob 0.45 (regulatory-receptivity coin-flip; serum AAT is a plausible surrogate but AA precedent for RNA-editing is thin). target_on_win $11 = de-risked path to a large AATD market (~200k pts US/EU), platform validation, re-rate toward 200-day $10 / prior GSK-partnered level. downside_on_loss $3.50 = cash floor ($544.6M cash, net cash $528.9M / ~192.4M sh = $2.75/sh) + residual platform/other programs (GSK 4th DC selected Jan 2026). BARBELL: +91% win vs -39% loss; EV@0.45 = 0.45*11+0.55*3.5 = $6.88 (+19%).
valuation.prob_basis: FDA-pathway-feedback binary (not approval): positive RestorAATion-2 surrogate biomarker data supports an AA case, but regulatory acceptance of serum AAT as a surrogate for a first-in-class RNA-editing agent is unproven — ~coin-flip, slightly below 50%.

valuation.valuation_basis: Merger arb — Axcelis (ACQUIRER) all-stock acquisition of Veeco, 0.3575 ACLS per VECO share (announced Oct 1, 2025; both shareholder bases approved Feb 6, 2026). Sole remaining condition: China SAMR clearance; guided close 2H 2026. Deal-implied VECO = 0.3575*$156.88 = $56.08 vs VECO spot $61.67 (market pricing ACLS upside through close). For ACLS itself the catalyst is deal CLOSE = accretive combination: $35M run-rate cost synergies (within 24mo), non-GAAP EPS accretive yr-1, 2024 pro-forma $1.7B rev / $387M adj EBITDA / 44% GM. fair_value_target $175 = post-close ACLS capitalizing synergies (~$35M*20x ~ $700M / ~33M pro-forma sh ~ +$21/sh) + removal of deal-uncertainty discount, off $156.88 spot (already +6.6% today on SAMR optimism). downside_floor $120 = standalone-on-break: a SAMR block gives back the recent deal/announcement premium spike but NOT to the pre-bid undisturbed (~$85-95) since ACLS tripled off $60 52wk-low largely on fundamental ion-implant/semi recovery. break_prob 0.25 (China SAMR geopolitics + Kingstone-competitor bundling concerns the live risk). reference_rr = (175-156.88)/(156.88-120) = 0.49; EV@close-prob 0.75 = 0.75*175+0.25*120 = $161.25 (+2.8%) — ACLS has run hard, asymmetry now thin.

valuation.valuation_basis: Single-asset developer NAV re-rate on a dated FID catalyst. Kabanga Ni-sulfide (LZM 84%, Tanzania govt 16% free-carry) July-2025 FS: after-tax NPV(8%) USD 1.58B @ $8.49/lb Ni, IRR 23.3%, AISC $3.36/lb, initial capex $942M (StockTitan/Mining.com 12/11/2025). Attributable NPV = 84% x $1,580M = $1,327M (after-tax, post-capex equity DCF). Apply 0.50x P/NAV post-FID/financing-close re-rate (single-asset, Tanzania country risk, financing/dilution overhang) booked as a signed risk discount of $664M. Net debt ~$30M (post $75M H2-25 raise + $60M bridge, less burn). FV = (1,327 - 30 - 664)/89.9 = $7.04. Aligns BTIG $7 Buy. Floor = stalled/un-financed developer at ~0.18x attributable NAV with dilution = ~$2.60 (below 52-wk low $3.07, embeds financing-failure de-rate).
valuation.valuation_method_note: supply_timing lane mapped to SoP/NAV: a single pre-FID development asset re-rating on a dated, forward, not-yet-fired catalyst (mid/late-2026 FID + financing close).

valuation.valuation_basis: BARBELL on FDA PMA decision Q4-2026 for Optune Lua/Mya in NSCLC brain metastases (METIS). METIS HIT primary endpoint: median time-to-intracranial-progression 21.9mo vs 11.3mo, HR 0.67, p=0.016 (n=298). BUT all key secondaries (OS, neurocognitive failure, radiologic response) MISSED significance and were planned labeling claims. Device PMA bar (primary met + safety, plus TTFields already FDA-approved in GBM/mesothelioma/met-NSCLC = strong precedent) -> win_prob 0.80. target_on_win $22.00: approval confirmed but brain-mets is a NARROW commercial opp (short Tx duration, poor prognosis; Lua+Pax guide only $15-25M 2026 rev) so re-rate is modest off $17.20 (mostly de-risks the multi-indication platform story; Street median PT $33.5 is a 12mo blended multi-indication case, not the event). downside_on_loss $13.50 on a CRL = revert toward 50d avg $14.26 / pre-run base, NOT cash: core GBM Optune business stands (~$600M+ rev run-rate, $441.8M cash+STI, net cash ~$207M). Source: Novocure METIS PR + ASTRO 2025; Q1-2026 8-K (cash). reference_rr = EV%.
valuation.prob_basis: win_prob 0.80: METIS primary endpoint met with p=0.016 + acceptable safety, and TTFields is already FDA-approved across 3 prior indications (regulatory precedent lowers device-PMA risk). Discount vs near-certainty reflects the OS/secondary-endpoint misses that were intended labeling claims. EV = 0.80*(22.00-17.20)+0.20*(13.50-17.20)= +$3.10 => +18.0%.

valuation.valuation_basis: Acquirer-side binary on the contested $22.00/sh cash, $6.2B Tegna acquisition (announced 19-Aug-2025). Deal 'closed' 19-Mar-2026 but ENJOINED 21-Apr-2026 (district court granted preliminary injunction finding likely Sec.7 antitrust harm); now in expedited 9th Circuit appeal. deal_price $255 = the level NXST re-rates to if injunction is vacated and deal stands: 40%+ FCF accretion + $300M synergies, ~7.0x pro-forma blended 2026-27 adj EBITDA ~$2.8B (Deutsche); FMP consensus PT $250, bullish set $270-300 (Guggenheim/Benchmark) -> use $255 (conservative-of-bull). undisturbed_price $165 = pre-bid standalone NXST (Q2'25 buybacks ~$159.71; standalone 2026 adj EBITDA $1.95-2.05B). Next hard date = 9th Circuit briefing completes late-Jun-2026, ruling 'well into fall 2026'. Source: CNBC/Deadline/Variety Apr-2026; Nexstar 19-Aug-2025 deal PR; Seeking Alpha accretion.
valuation.prob_basis: break_prob 0.55 (more-likely-than-not to fail): a granted antitrust preliminary injunction is hard to overturn on appeal, the competitive harm finding is substantive, and even a win means trial not certain close; partly offset by FCC ownership-cap tailwinds. Market ($177 vs $255 win/$165 floor) implies only ~13% prob of full win — I assign higher legal odds but still net break-skewed.

catalyst_3.title: Verify
catalyst_3.evidence: STRUCTURE CORRECTION (primary 8-Ks): The "tax-free separation into TWO PUBLIC COMPANIES by mid-2026 (NewCo Urology/AcuteCare/OEM stub + RemainCo) PLUS two separate divestitures" framing is stale/wrong. The original Feb-2025 spin-NewCo plan was SUPERSEDED in Dec-2025: the would-be-NewCo assets are now being SOLD FOR CASH, not spun. Per SEC 8-Ks, Teleflex signed definitive agreements (Dec 2025) to sell Acute Care + Interventional Urology to Intersurgical (~$530M) and OEM to Montagu/Kohlberg (~$1.5B), ~$2.03B total, closing 2H26 (regulatory approval the remaining gate). So there is NO NewCo public stub / SOTP two-company structure and the Form 10 / IRS PLR / tax-free-distribution gates the LINE cites DO NOT apply (taxable cash sale). valuation.valuation_basis: BARBELL (not a ratio). Catalyst = Phase 2 RADIANT topline for ACP-204 (remlifanserin), a selective 5-HT2A inverse agonist, in Alzheimer's Disease Psychosis (ADP); ~318 pts, 30/60mg vs placebo, primary = SAPS-H+D at Wk6; topline Aug-Oct 2026, reaffirmed Q1 release (8-K 2026-05-06) and at RBC (5/19) & BofA (5/12). Remlifanserin = ~$4B of ACADIA's ~$11B unadj peak-sales pipeline. PROB BASIS: mixed precedent for 5-HT2A in ADP -- HARMONY (relapse-prevention) hit but FDA rejected the DRP sNDA (2021), and prior ADP acute-treatment efficacy in AD-specific cohorts underwhelmed; CNS/AD Ph2 acute-efficacy base rate is low. New, cleaner Ph2 design + selective molecule, but still a hard binary readout -> win_prob 0.35 (calibrated). target_on_win 31.0 (positive Ph2 de-risks the ~$4B asset -> re-rate toward 52-wk high $28.35 + pipeline credit). downside_on_loss 16.5 -- NOT a cash shell: 171.3M sh, Nuplazid+Daybue commercial base + ~$700M cash floor the equity below the 52-wk low $19.69 but a clean miss on a flagship asset re-rates to base-business value ~$16-17. reference_rr is EV%: EV=0.35*31+0.65*16.5=$21.58 vs ref 21.33 (~+1.2% upside) for ~-23% downside -> rr ~0.05; market is fairly/efficiently pricing this binary.
valuation.prob_basis: Mixed 5-HT2A-in-ADP precedent: HARMONY relapse-prevention hit but DRP sNDA FDA-rejected 2021; acute ADP efficacy in AD-specific cohorts historically weak; CNS Ph2 acute-efficacy base rate ~25-35%. New selective molecule + cleaner 6-wk design nudges up -> 0.35.

(2) Core Bio-Rad (Life Science $0.9B + Clinical Dx $1.5B rev; subscale 10-12% op margin = the activist thesis) adj EBITDA ~$460M x 10x (below-peer-margin life-science tools comp; higher-quality peers 14-18x) = $4,600M. Sum EV $9,600M; net cash $186M (debt $1,378M vs cash+STI $1,565M); tax leakage on low-basis Sartorius monetization ~$1,125M (25% on ~$4.5B gain) = the very reason the market discounts the stake. FV = (9,600 +186 -1,125)/27.0 = $320.8 (~10% above conservative SoP). Floor = Elliott fails, stake stays trapped + margins subscale -> pre-campaign de-rate ~$235 (above $218 low, strips the 5/18 activist pop).

valuation.valuation_basis: Bio binary BARBELL on PIVOT-006 Phase 3 topline (adjuvant intravesical cretostimogene vs surveillance in intermediate-risk NMIBC, RFS primary endpoint, N~364 1:1 open-label, 90+ sites, enrollment completed Sep 2025; topline accelerated to H1 2026 per GlobeNewswire/IR 2026-01-09). win_prob = 0.62 (CALIBRATED): the cretostimogene molecule is already de-risked by POSITIVE pivotal Phase 3 BOND-003 data in HR-NMIBC (high complete-response rates), the IR comparator is mere SURVEILLANCE (a weak control that favors the active arm), and the endpoint is RFS in a well-powered registrational design — together pushing PoS above the ~30-40% Phase-3-oncology base rate. target_on_win $78 (clean PIVOT-006 win + rolling HR-NMIBC BLA momentum re-rates toward/through the $75.5 prior 52-wk high). downside_on_loss $32 — NOT bare cash ($1.1B / 88.2M sh = ~$12.5/sh) because a PIVOT-006-only miss leaves the HR-NMIBC BOND-003 franchise + BLA intact; $32 = cash floor + retained HR program value. EV = 0.62*78 + 0.38*32 = $60.5 vs $53.95 ref -> +12.2% expected value (reference_rr stored as EV%).
valuation.prob_basis: Positive pivotal BOND-003 Phase 3 in HR-NMIBC de-risks the same molecule; surveillance-only comparator in PIVOT-006 favors the active arm; well-powered RFS registrational design (N~364) -> calibrated PoS ~0.62, above the generic ~0.35 Phase-3-onc base rate.

valuation.prob_basis: Oncology Ph3 base rate ~40-50%; real Ph1 PFS signal + WEAK active comparators (belinostat/pralatrexate ORR ~25-27%) help, but small single-arm Ph1, PFS-only, niche r/r PTCL temper it -> calibrated 0.40.
valuation.company_note: BARBELL not ratio. Win ($24/sh): de-risked PTCL orphan drug (peak ~$300-500M niche) + atopic-dermatitis Ph1-positive optionality -> $1.5-2.5B EV takeout/standalone (yrHigh $26.95 reflects prior optimism). Loss ($2.85/sh): trial fail -> ~cash/share ($236.7M/84M=$2.82), AD program adds minimal residual. Expected EV = 0.40x$24 + 0.60x$2.85 = $9.60+$1.71 = $11.31 vs ref $11.17 -> ~+1% (binary EV%). Essentially FAIRLY PRICED at $11.17; edge only if you handicap PoS >0.40 or value AD optionality higher. reference_rr shown as EV% (+0.01).

valuation.valuation_basis: BARBELL. Catalyst = pre-specified DSMB interim on first 200 patients (75%/~150 enrolled per StockTitan/BioSpace; completion guided by end-2026) in ReMEDy2 (NCT05065216), an adaptive Ph2/3 of DM199 (rinvecalinase alfa) in acute ischemic stroke. NOTE the interim is a sample-size re-estimation / futility gate, NOT an efficacy success readout (final N 300-728), so 'win' = clears futility with a favorable signal (no large up-sizing), not approval. win_prob 0.55 (continuation likely but a strongly positive non-SSR outcome is rarer; AIS neuroprotection PoS-to-approval historically ~10-15%). target_on_win $10 re-rate (~$540M cap, prior 52-wk high $10.42, 200-day $7.18). downside_on_loss $2 (futility/large up-size toward cash; Q1-26 cash+STI $51.3M / 53.8M sh = $0.95/sh, plus residual DM199 CKD/PNH optionality). EV = 0.55*10 + 0.45*2 = $6.4 vs $5.54 ref = +15.5% (binary EV%).
valuation.prob_basis: Adaptive Ph2/3 interim is a continuation/futility gate (DSMB SSR), not go/no-go efficacy; base-rate for 'not stopped + no large up-size' ~0.55. Acute ischemic stroke neuroprotection has a long graveyard (PoS-to-approval ~10-15%); de-risking signal weighted but the binary here is the interim itself. Source: DiaMedica PR / BioSpace May-2026 75% enrollment milestone; FY2025 results.

valuation.valuation_basis: Merger-arb (KMB/Kenvue). Per-share consideration = $3.50 cash + 0.14625 KMB; at KMB $98.76 that is $3.50 + 0.14625*98.76 = $17.94. Fixed exchange ratio means KVUE holders bear full KMB downside: deal was worth $21.01 at announce (KMB $115.10, Oct-31-2025) and has bled to ~$17.94 as KMB fell to ~$99. Shareholders approved Jan-29-2026 (99% KVUE / 96% KMB), US HSR cleared Feb-4-2026, but FTC issued a SECOND REQUEST, EC FSR provisional deadline Jun-8-2026, and China SAMR opened a deeper review (asked for more info, May-29-2026). Outside date Nov-2-2026, auto-extends to May-3-2027 for regulatory. Expected close H2-2026. Tylenol/autism litigation is the standalone overhang. Source: Kenvue 8-K (kvue-20260129), KMB 8-K (May-2026), MLex (EU FSR), Seeking Alpha (SAMR), DEFM14A.
valuation.prob_basis: Break ~12%: binding agreement + both shareholder votes + HSR clearance make a walk unlikely; residual risk is a remote SAMR/EC block or a litigation-driven MAC. Note the bigger risk is not a BREAK but further KMB-equity erosion (80% of consideration is stock) — that lowers realized value even on a clean close.

valuation.valuation_basis: SoP of out-of-favor met-coal core (trough) + risk-weighted Brook Mine REE optionality. Met coal: normalized mid-cycle EBITDA ~$60M (FY25 Adj EBITDA $36.1M; Q1-26 Adj EBITDA -$1.8M trough; FY26 guide 3.7-4.1Mt prod at thin $16/t cash margin) at 4.5x small-cap met-coal comp = $270M EV. Brook Mine REE: Fluor PEA (Jul-2025) pre-tax NPV8 $1.197B; post-tax ~$0.88B, but MOUs (REalloys/Mulberry) are NON-BINDING and Phase-1 volumes are late-2026/2027, so risk-weighted at 0.25 readiness = $220M. Net CASH $304M (Q1-26 BS). 53.8M sh. Build = (270+220+304)/53.8 = $14.76. Floor strips ALL REE credit (coal-only de-rated): (270+304)/53.8 ~ $10.7, taken to ~$9.50 on a deeper coal trough.

sop_components.basis: Normalized mid-cycle EBITDA above FY25 Adj $36.1M trough / Q1-26 -$1.8M; 4.5x vs small-cap met-coal peers (CEIX/AMR/HCC trade ~3-5x). PR 2026-05-11 FY26 guide 3.7-4.1Mt, $95-100/t cash cost.

Fluor independent PEA 2025-07-02 pre-tax NPV8 $1.197B (post-tax ~$0.88B), initial capex $473M; 0.25 readiness/probability factor as REalloys+Mulberry+DOE/NETL ties are NON-BINDING MOUs and Phase-1 (525tpa NdPr) is late-2026/2027.

valuation.valuation_basis: Barbell on obicetrapib (CETP inhibitor). LDL franchise already de-risked: BROADWAY/BROOKLYN/TANDEM Phase 3 positive (35-50% LDL-C lowering), EMA MAA VALIDATED/accepted (Menarini-filed), EMA/UK/Swiss decisions 2H26. The multi-billion binary is PREVAIL CVOT (>9,500 pts, MACE) -> interim 4Q26, results pushed to 1Q27. NAMS retains US economics (the prize); EU via Menarini royalties (low-double to mid-20s% + up to EUR863M milestones). Cash $707.3M (3/31/26), 116.9M sh (~$6/sh net cash). WIN (EU approval + supportive PREVAIL -> obicetrapib becomes a CV-outcomes statin-adjunct, 7MM peak ~$1.4B+, US-owned): re-rate to ~$60 (top of analyst range $37.77-$59.33; Street median $47.51; William Blair FV $43; one analyst 90% PoS, >100% upside). LOSE (PREVAIL fails to show CV benefit -> niche LDL-lowering only, value collapses toward cash + modest EU royalty stream): ~$14. win_prob 0.55 calibrated: near-term EU approval ~90% (drug works, MAA validated) but the price-defining CVOT leg is a true coin-flip-plus (CETP class has a graveyard, but obicetrapib has clean LDL + ApoB data and ongoing Alzheimer's biomarker signal). EV = 0.55*60 + 0.45*14 = ~$39, below spot $31 only if you weight downside heavier; at 0.55 EV>spot.
valuation.prob_basis: Blended: ~90% near-term EU/UK/Swiss approval (Phase 3 LDL data positive + EMA MAA already validated) x ~0.6 conditional on the value-defining PREVAIL CVOT showing MACE benefit (CETP-class historical failures offset by obicetrapib's clean LDL/ApoB profile and Alzheimer's biomarker signal); one sell-side analyst assigns 90% PoS. Calibrated to 0.55 for the full re-rate.

valuation.valuation_basis: SPAC cash-box arb. NPAC (def. BCA 16-Mar-2026) takes Abra Financial (digital-asset wealth platform) public at $750M pre-money as ABRX on Nasdaq; $40M minimum-net-cash close condition; Abra holders to own majority. IPO put $10.05/unit in trust (Apr-2025); pro-rata redemption ~$10.33 at 12/31/25, accreting ~5%/yr -> ~$10.45 by mid-2026. NPAC trades $10.37 ~ trust. Protection = redemption right: a holder at/below trust gets ~$10.40 back if the deal breaks or they redeem, so 'break' is not a loss -> fair_value_target set just above trust ($10.60) for deal-completion optionality on a speculative crypto de-SPAC (these rarely sustain premiums post-close); downside_floor = redemption value $10.40. Asymmetric but tiny absolute spread; the warrant (1/3, $11.50 strike) is the free option, not modeled in equity floor.

valuation.valuation_basis: EHA2026 oral on Jun-14-26 is an INCREMENTAL same-trial Phase 1a/b CLL update for bexobrutideg (NX-5948), NOT an approval binary, so this is a soft event-weighted re-rate, not a coin-flip. Anchor: ASH Dec-2025 showed ~81% ORR (2 CRs) in heavily pretreated R/R CLL, median PFS 22.1m; Roche validated mechanism with $700M upfront / up to $2.3B deal (clinicaltrialvanguard, 2025). Sell-side targets cluster $30-36 (Morgan Stanley OW $36, Stifel $33, HCW $31, Mizuho $30; investing.com 2026). WIN (good/confirmatory RP2D data) re-rates toward lower-half of that range with a haircut for incremental nature -> target_on_win $24. LOSS (in-line/soft print) drifts back to pre-run support ~$11 (NOT cash $3.7/sh: co. is well-funded $320M and Roche-backed, so floor is trading support not liquidation). EV%: 0.45*(24/15.61-1)+0.55*(11/15.61-1)=+0.080.
valuation.prob_basis: Incremental same-trial confirmatory readout (not a registrational binary); calibrated ~0.45 that updated EHA data is a clear positive surprise vs in-line. Mechanism already de-risked by ASH-2025 efficacy + Roche $2.3B partnership; risk is the print is merely confirmatory and the run-up (yearLow 8.20 -> 15.61) unwinds.

valuation.valuation_basis: OVERRIDE of clean merger-arb: this is a TRANSFORMATIVE REVERSE-STYLE COMBINATION, not a UROY buyout (triage flag confirmed). UROY to combine with Sweetwater Royalties (92% from Orion + Ontario Teachers') into newly-formed 'New URC', NASDAQ-listed; arrangement agreement 4/16/2026; shareholder vote ~July-2026 requiring 66-2/3% of URC votes (SEC 6-K; StockTitan). Consideration to sellers: $330M cash + $813M stock issued at $3.64/sh New URC. Sweetwater EV ~$1.9B incl ~$625M debt -> combined entity materially MORE LEVERED; existing URC holders end at only ~41% pro-forma (Orion 43%, OTPP 16%) -> NOT accretive cash-out; holders roll into a larger, geared vehicle. NO fixed cash consideration to arb -> this is a pro-forma NAV re-rate. Anchor: struck New URC share value $3.64 (best 'deal' reference). KEY TELL: stock FELL on announcement (undisturbed 4/15/2026 close $3.92 -> $3.76 -> $3.63, now $3.205), i.e. the market is pricing dilution/leverage/execution risk; current price sits BELOW both the strike and the undisturbed level. fair_value_target=$3.64 (struck pro-forma share value); downside_floor=$2.90 (deal-fail/uranium-soft haircut toward 52wk-low $2.20 zone). NOTE undisturbed=$3.92 sits ABOVE the strike -> negative information content; reference_rr=(3.64-3.205)/(3.205-2.90)=1.43 is thin and overhung by dilution.
valuation.prob_basis: Needs 66-2/3% URC shareholder vote; existing holders only ~41% pro-forma and stock trading below the $3.64 strike signals ambivalence toward a more-levered reverse combination -> elevated break/renegotiation risk ~25%

Prob-weighted EV = 0.58*$20 + 0.42*$5.50 = $13.91 vs ref $8.49 (+64%). reference_rr 5.3 = prob-weighted reward/risk: 0.58*(20-8.49) / 0.42*(8.49-5.50).

Floor $2.50 = cyclical-trough equity if dayrates stay soft (5x on ~$475M trough EBITDA leaves only ~$0.55/sh deep-trough; $2.50 reflects refi-de-risked low-end trading, vs 52wk low $1.79). valuation.valuation_basis: Partial-separation SoP. MMS marked at the hard Apollo third-party comp: $1.25B convertible preferred for ~13% closed Jun-1-2026 implying ~$13.0B MMS EV (businesswire/mckesson PR Jun-1-2026; MMS FY26 rev $11.5B, adj seg op profit $1.0B => ~13x, a specialty/med-distribution premium). Build: NA Pharmaceutical adj op profit $3.5B x12.0x ($42.0B; Cencora COR ~12-13x EV/EBITDA comp), Oncology&Multispecialty $1.4B x13.0x ($18.2B; specialty/US Oncology Network premium), Rx Technology Solutions $1.1B x15.0x ($16.5B; tech-enabled access/CoverMyMeds software-services comp), MMS $13.0B (Apollo mark). RESULT: trades ABOVE the disciplined SoP at $766.64 -> NO_UPSIDE; the Apollo/IPO catalyst is already in the price and MCK already carries a premium ~13.4x consolidated EV/EBITDA. Floor = de-rate to ~11x blended distribution multiple if MMS IPO is pulled, ~$560 (below the $637 52-wk low, an honest no-deal de-rate for a name at all-time-high multiples).

valuation.valuation_basis: BARBELL on rezpegaldesleukin (Treg-stimulating IL-2 conjugate). The 4/20/2026 REZOLVE-AA 52-wk catalyst is STALE (stock already spiked then pulled back from $77 to $56). Forward binary = Phase 3 ZENITH-AD (atopic dermatitis, initiating Q2'26) success. win_prob 0.45 CALIBRATED: dermatology Phase 3 PoS base rate ~55% given a positive Phase 2b, discounted for a competitive efficacy bar vs IL-4/13 incumbents (Dupixent/lebrikizumab/nemolizumab) and single-asset concentration. target_on_win $140 = de-risked multi-indication (AD+AA) franchise PV, vs sell-side median $136.5 (BTIG $151, HCW $165, B.Riley $150). downside_on_loss $22 = net-cash floor (~$725M raised across 2026 offerings, ~19.5M sh) + residual AA optionality. EV = 0.45*140 + 0.55*22 = $75.1 vs $56.10 = +34%.
valuation.prob_basis: Immunology/dermatology Phase 3 historical PoS ~50-60% after a positive Phase 2b (BIO/Informa clinical-success rates); rezpeg Phase 2b REZOLVE-AD (393 pts) showed solid but not class-leading EASI-75/90, hence 0.45 not 0.55. Jefferies independently models AA at 20% PoS / $300M peak, underscoring single-indication binary risk.

valuation.valuation_basis: Standalone re-rate (single operating entity) - OPFI is the ACQUIRER of BNCCORP/BNC National Bank (~$130M cash+stock, def. agreement Apr-28/29-2026, eliminates the Up-C, Q4-2026 close pending OCC/Fed/FDIC + BNCC vote; PRNewswire/OppFi IR). There is NO take-out for OPFI holders, so this is a standalone P/E re-rate, not a spread. OPFI = profitable high-ROE subprime online installment lender: FY25 adj EPS $1.59 / adj NI $139.8M; FY26 guide adj EPS $1.76-1.84 (mid ~$1.80) / adj NI $153-160M on ~87M dil sh. At $7.84 it trades ~4.4x FY26E adj EPS. Owning a national bank charter de-risks the rent-a-bank model and is +25%/+40% EPS-accretive 2027/2028. Fair value = a disciplined 6.4x FY26E adj EPS (mid NI ~$156.6M) capitalized = ~$1,003M equity / 85.4M econ sh = $11.74 - haircut vs a normal ~7-8x lender multiple for the genuine high-APR CFPB/state rate-cap regulatory tail. Corporate net debt excluded (OPFI's debt funds the loan book; metric is a P/E-derived equity mark). RESULT: $11.75 vs $7.84 = real upside, the one name with edge - but floor held tight at $6.00 (subprime credit-cycle/regulatory de-rate, ~4x mid-cycle EPS, below the $7.36 52-wk low) per the 'edge mispriced' caution.

catalyst_2.title: Milestone
catalyst_2.description: China SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) clearance / deal close — expected H2-2026; next hard gate is SAMR sign-off (no fixed date, both parties reaffirmed H2-2026 as of the Feb 6, 2026 vote).

catalyst_density.rating: L — the convergence edge is inverted and the main de-risking event already fired
catalyst_density.analysis: The deal is real, dated, and specific (definitive all-stock merger, 0.3575 ACLS/VECO, both shareholder votes passed Feb 6 2026, only China SAMR left, close H2-2026), but the headline arb catalyst has largely FIRED: votes are done and a single foreign regulator remains. Critically, at the Jun 6 2026 close ACLS $147.16 × 0.3575 = $52.61 implied vs VECO $58.45, so the spread is NEGATIVE ~-10% — VECO trades 11% ABOVE deal value, meaning a standard long-VECO arb loses on convergence and the market is implicitly pricing a topping bid or deal-break re-rate. With the conventional edge inverted and only binary China-regulatory risk left, this is a WATCH (track SAMR headlines), not an actionable ACTIVE long-arb.

Deal value = 0.3575 x ACLS $157.00 = $56.13/sh. candidates.instrument: stock (NYSE: VSXY, formerly VSCO; ticker changed June 2, 2026)
candidates.analysis_summary: [SWEEP 19 (UCB-WEX)] The catalyst is real, specific, dated and forward: a named 13% activist (BBRC) with a filed definitive proxy targeting the Chair at a June 11, 2026 vote (G1 satisfied), so it clears the hard gates. But the unpriced asymmetry is weak — all three independent proxy advisors backed the incumbent board on June 3, making activist defeat the likely outcome, and the shares have already run hard (50dMA ~$50 to ~$74.60), so a board win on June 11 is more "uncertainty removed" than a re-rating driver. With no board concession (G2) or specific unpriced figure (G3) and a depleted edge, this is a WATCH/event-risk item rather than an ACTIVE long; triage's ACTIVE/6 overstates given the advisor sweep and prior re-rate.

Sources: BBRC DFAN14A, VSCO Q1-FY26 10-Q (net debt), stocktitan Jun-2026 (3 proxy advisors FOR board).

valuation.valuation_basis: Forced-seller = monetizing the Whirlpool-of-India stake to delever. The Nov-2025 leg (51%->39.76%, deconsolidation, ~$166M + $251M gain) already fired; the dead Advent '$550-600M/31%' plan collapsed Dec-2025 on valuation, but a LIVE auction continues (EQT/Bain lead bidders, binding offers ~Aug, WHR retaining ~20%). Proceeds go to debt paydown (NAV-neutral). SoP: core appliances (ex-India) on a NORMALIZED EBITDA ~$1,300M (partial recovery off the 4% trough margin toward 9% mid-cycle; FY26 guide is trough: ~$15.0B sales x ~4% EBIT + D&A) @5.5x (Electrolux/appliance peers 5-6x) = $7,150M; residual ~40% listed India stake at market ($1,375M full India equity x 0.40) = $550M; sum EV $7,700M less net debt $6,478M (Q1-26) less pension/OPEB underfunding ~$300M = $922M /64.8 sh = $14.23. At $39.56 the market prices a FULL mid-cycle (9%-margin) recovery on a thin levered equity sliver (net debt $6.5B vs $2.6B cap, EV ~9.6x fwd EBITDA) -> NO_UPSIDE. Sources: MINING.COM/SEC 8-K (Nov-2025 sell-down), Business Standard Jul-2026 (EQT/Bain), WHR Q1-26 slides + FY26 guide (4% EBIT, FCF >$300M, net-debt <$5B target), Whirlpool-of-India NSE mkt cap.

valuation.valuation_basis: Barbell — the asymmetric Ph3 readout is BEHIND us and captured. X-TOLE2 (focal-onset seizures) HIT positive 9-Mar-2026 (-53.2%/-34.5% median FOS reduction at 25mg/15mg vs -10.4% placebo); stock spiked to $63.95, faded to $51.27 (GlobeNewswire 9-Mar; NeurologyLive). Remaining binary = FDA approval + launch: NDA Q3-2026, market late-2027/early-2028. win_prob 0.85 (de-risked: TWO positive pivotals X-TOLE + X-TOLE2, clean safety, KV7 mechanism). target_on_win $74 = sell-side Buy target on peak sales $2bn+ ('conservative' per Jefferies; +bipolar/MDD optionality). downside_on_loss $18 ~ net cash $1.33bn / ~79M sh = ~$17 floor on a CRL. Prob-weighted EV = 0.85x$74 + 0.15x$18 = $65.6 vs $51.27 spot => +28% expected (EV%).

recent_events.title: Ziff Davis signed a definitive deal to sell its Connectivity division (Ookla/Speedtest/Ekahau/Downdetector/RootMetrics) to Accenture for $1.2B cash, with a broader exploratory strategic review for further asset sales/spin-offs still open.
recent_events.link: Ziff Davis 8-K / BusinessWire "Ziff Davis Announces Definitive Agreement to Sell Connectivity Division to Accenture" (Mar 2, 2026; SEC EDGAR CIK 0001084048 filed Mar 3, 2026); strategic-review exploratory language confirmed via Simply Wall St / Q1-2026 commentary (May 2026).

valuation.valuation_basis: Value-surfacing catalyst (activist Pale Fire Capital 6.9% on capitalization/board; no public PT) = the definitive $1.2B-cash sale of Connectivity (Ookla/Speedtest/Ekahau/Downdetector/RootMetrics) to Accenture, announced Mar 3 2026, closing 'in coming months'. Post-close SoP: RemainCo adj EBITDA ~$261M (FY25 total adj EBITDA $381.4M less Connectivity ~$120M at its >50% margin on $231M rev) @6.0x (ZD blended currently ~5.4x; modest re-rate for the cleaner, buyback-fueled story, discounted for AI-search/secular decline at PCMag/Mashable/IGN) = $1,566M EV. Pro-forma the balance sheet to NET CASH: current net debt $347M (Q1-26) less ~$950M net Accenture proceeds ($1.2B less ~$250M tax on low-basis assets) = -$603M net cash. (1,566 - (-603))/36.84 sh = $58.88. Genuine residual upside vs $46.60: market still values the whole co ~5.4x and under-credits the net-cash-rich RemainCo re-rate. 25M-sh buyback authorization ($51.6M used Q1-26) supports. Sources: ZD 8-K Mar-3-2026 (Accenture $1.2B), ZD 4Q25 release (adj EBITDA $381.4M), Q1-25 slides (Connectivity >50% EBITDA margin), ZD 10-Q (36.84M sh, net debt).
